title: Repository State, References, and Revision Algebra · History Defense
summary: A maintainer review compares near-miss operations for repository state, references, and revision algebra and requires the smallest safe repository-state and revision-query proof with topology and content evidence. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for repository-state and revision-query proof. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Distinguish direct refs, symbolic refs, pseudorefs, remote-tracking refs, packed refs, and detached HEAD using plumbing evidence.
Resolve revision names and disambiguate refs, object IDs, paths, reflog selectors, and peel operators before an operation.
Construct inclusive, exclusive, symmetric-difference, ancestry, parent, and path-limited revision sets for an exact question.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-advanced cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Diagnose the hidden failure
steps.instruction:
quiz case — Case G89 is a quiz in a disposable repository. the audit mismatch is confined to commits reachable from exactly one reviewed tip. The learner must predict left and right markers expose exclusive ancestry without moving a reference, run git rev-list --left-right --ancestry-path release...topic -- src/policy.ts, explain two right-only policy commits and one left-only release fix define the review set, test this changed condition: replace topic with topic^{tree} and reject the query because a tree has no commit ancestry, and preserve this recovery boundary: read-only query; retain refs/audit/before-query at the reviewed release tip.

The visible result looks correct, but its invariant or value contract is unverified. What must happen next?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for capture repository boundaries, object format, refs, worktrees, index state, operation state, and recovery anchors before investigation.

options.text: Inspect repository boundary, config origin, current operation, worktree, index, refs, and graph
Preserve uncommitted work and a recovery ref, then state intended tree and topology
Preview and perform the least destructive simulated Git operation
Verify status, staged and range diffs, graph, tests, remote target, and recovery path

steps.title: Defend one difficult decision
steps.instruction: Write the quiz evidence note for Gita Readonly Forensic Baseline: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — Case G96 is a quiz in a disposable repository. the audit mismatch is confined to commits reachable from exactly one reviewed tip. The learner must predict left and right markers expose exclusive ancestry without moving a reference, run git rev-list --left-right --ancestry-path release...topic -- src/policy.ts, explain two right-only policy commits and one left-only release fix define the review set, test this changed condition: replace topic with topic^{tree} and reject the query because a tree has no commit ancestry, and preserve this recovery boundary: read-only query; retain refs/audit/before-query at the reviewed release tip.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Capture repository boundaries, object format, refs, worktrees, index state, operation state, and recovery anchors before investigation.
